title: grok-swarm.yaml
description: Official JSON Schema for grok-swarm.yaml — defines a coordinated multi-agent swarm with named members, a coordinator (or peer mode), communication topology, consensus rule, and explicit fallback behaviour. Introduced in grok-yaml-standards@2.0. Compatible with grok-install.yaml@1.0+ and grok@2026.4+.

version.description: Semantic version of this swarm configuration file.

author.description: X handle of the swarm config owner, prefixed with '@'.

compatibility.description: Platform specs this file is tested and compatible with.

id.description: Stable machine identifier used in '@grok spawn swarm:<id>' triggers.

name.description: Human-readable swarm name shown in dashboards and logs.

version.description: Semver of the swarm definition itself (independent of the file version).

description.description: One- or two-sentence summary of the swarm's purpose.

members.description: Ordered list of agent members. Each member references an agent defined in grok-agent.yaml.

agent_id.description: Identifier of an agent defined in grok-agent.yaml.

role.description: Swarm role played by this member.

priority.description: Tiebreaker weight when electing a new coordinator. Higher wins.

enabled.description: Whether this member participates. Set to false to disable without removing the entry.

coordinator.description: agent_id of the lead agent, or the literal 'none' for peer swarms.

communication.description: Message-passing topology used between swarm members.

consensus.description: Decision rule when members disagree on a shared action.

fallback.description: Failure-handling policy for member and coordinator failures.

strategy.description: What to do when a member fails.

reassign_to.description: agent_id to take over when strategy is 'reassign'.

metadata.description: Free-form map of tags, owners, lineage. Values may be strings, numbers, booleans, or arrays of those.
